formal_profiles.description: Development-focused formal profile: EDGRec over the practical core thesis datasets with explicit 1/2 branch depth and the default observed train-interaction graph.

Default compact EDGRec search prior and low-risk formal candidate. This is not a final thesis selection: KuaiRec_v2 has the strongest compact accuracy/speed evidence, MovieLens1M is a near-parity/speed candidate with weaker popularity diagnostics, and KuaiRand1K is diagnostic/stress-test evidence. AmazonBook is excluded only from this shared compact queue; it remains an EDGRec optimization target through dataset-specific compact and deeper/feature candidate profiles plus LightGCN-paper accuracy framing.

AmazonBook-specific compact graph-only EDGRec candidate. This profile is excluded from the shared compact default queue but keeps AmazonBook inside EDGRec optimization for compact-vs-deep comparison before thesis promotion.

AmazonBook EDGRec formal candidate preserved from prior Optuna evidence: deeper 2/3 branch propagation, explicit side features with a controlled feature gate, and bounded contrastive loss. This profile is an AmazonBook-specific candidate to compare against the compact EDGRec candidate and LightGCN-paper accuracy baseline, not part of the compact default search prior.
Core EDGRec thesis mainline across practical datasets. Paper baselines are split into separate run-once profiles so EDGRec tuning can iterate without rerunning fixed paper defaults.
Dataset-local lightweight EDGRec profile for KuaiRec_v2 big-matrix/watch-ratio thesis runs.
Dataset-local lightweight EDGRec profile for MovieLens1M secondary sanity runs.
Dataset-local lightweight EDGRec diagnostic profile for KuaiRand-1K label and randomized-exposure checks.
Controlled video-dataset EDGRec baseline for the long/short temporal-interest ablation. Temporal interest is explicitly disabled so this row can be compared against the matching temporal profiles.
Small temporal long/short-interest EDGRec ablation for video datasets. It keeps the EDGRec architecture fixed and adds only the gated recent-history interest path.
Temporal long/short-interest EDGRec ablation with the item-only context head disabled. Use it to test whether temporal interest replaces or complements context/popularity scoring.

Core EDGRec thesis mainline across practical datasets. Paper baselines are split into separate run-once profiles so EDGRec tuning can iterate without rerunning fixed paper defaults. Opposite fan-out from the default (smaller for small datasets, larger for medium datasets) and smaller learning rate to check whether the default settings are actually optimal before committing to long runs.

NDCG@20 follow-up for AmazonBook: rerun the older high-NDCG EDGRec mainline basin with graph-only inputs, smaller batch size, learned fusion that may collapse toward interest, and the 10-5 fan-out that was closer to the best historical EDGRec ranking row.

NDCG@20 follow-up for AmazonBook: EDGRec reduced toward an interest-only LightGCN-like scorer with paper-style 3-hop fan-out, testing whether the extra conformity/context channels are what cost ranking accuracy on graph-only data.
NDCG@20 follow-up for AmazonBook: graph-only EDGRec with wider 20-10 sampling to test whether the paper LightGCN gap is mostly neighbor coverage rather than objective choice.
NDCG@20 follow-up for MovieLens1M: reproduce the near-parity core EDGRec basin but remove genre features, because feature-subset evidence shows MovieLens genres reduce validation utility.
NDCG@20 follow-up for MovieLens1M: keep EDGRec's branch architecture but force an interest-heavy fixed score mix to test whether the global-top fixed balanced mix was suppressing ranking accuracy.

NDCG@20 follow-up for MovieLens1M: EDGRec reduced to an interest-only sampled LightGCN-like scorer under the MovieLens explicit split, testing whether model complexity is unnecessary on dense explicit ratings.
NDCG@20 follow-up for KuaiRec_v2: take the best current global-top accuracy basin and enable only the strongest validation feature group, item_resolution.
NDCG@20 follow-up for KuaiRec_v2: same global-top r3 basin, but enable only item_video_metadata, the second strongest single feature group in validation.
NDCG@20 follow-up for KuaiRec_v2: deeper r4 basin with the strongest pair feature subset, item_video_metadata plus item_resolution.

NDCG@20 follow-up for KuaiRec_v2: explicitly rerun the old high-NDCG 20-10 feature-rich ablation shape under the current kuairec_watchratio preset, so the suspicious older rows are tested on the current split contract.
NDCG@20 follow-up for KuaiRand1K: move out of the ultra-compact random-exposure item universe and test the best validation side-feature triple under a standard observed-item universe.

NDCG@20 follow-up for KuaiRand1K: standard observed-item universe with an interest-heavy EDGRec scorer, testing whether the compact global-top rows lost NDCG because they over-prioritized randomized-exposure efficiency.
NDCG@20 follow-up for KuaiRand1K: keep the cheap global-top r5 randomized-exposure contract but add the best feature triple from the feature-subset search.
Run-once paper-faithful LightGCN baselines for the smaller practical datasets so finished baseline metrics are available before committing to large video runs.
Run-once paper-faithful LightGCN baselines on all practical core datasets after the small runs and large-dataset timing probes justify the resource cost.
One-epoch full-data timing probes for paper LightGCN on the large video datasets. These rows are for runtime estimation, not final thesis metrics.
One-epoch full-data timing probes for paper GCN-DICE on Movielens1M, AmazonBook, KuaiRec, and KuaiRand. These rows document feasibility and should not be interpreted as final ranking metrics.

Hardware-scaled LightGCN paper-architecture baseline for AmazonBook and MovieLens1M, where current full rows make this comparison most useful. This preserves the paper adapter, loss, optimizer, scheduler, and full-graph evaluation path, but enables auto batch-size selection; report separately from paper-faithful reproduction rows.

Hardware-scaled GCN-DICE feasibility probe on MovieLens1M. Same paper-DICE architecture, loss, DICE sampler, and full-graph training path, but auto-selects a larger batch to test whether small-batch overhead drives the runtime bottleneck.
Hardware-scaled GCN-DICE feasibility probe on AmazonBook. Run only after the MovieLens1M scaled-batch probe shows meaningful runtime improvement.
Fallback profile for faster sampled LightGCN and legacy DICE-like baselines if paper-faithful full-graph baselines are infeasible. Report these separately from paper-default baselines.
EDGRec-only deeper-branch comparison for checking whether interest=2 and conformity=3 help before spending full stress-run time.
Movielens1M EDGRec learning-rate schedule sweep. Paper baselines keep their own fixed defaults and are not rerun here.

Long unattended EDGRec follow-up across the practical core datasets with the current branch asymmetry (interest=1, conformity=2), larger 20-10 fan-out, cosine scheduling, and a 300-epoch budget guarded by early stopping.

Long unattended EDGRec follow-up across the practical core datasets with the current branch asymmetry (interest=1, conformity=2), 8-4 fan-out, cosine scheduling, and a 300-epoch budget guarded by early stopping.
Taobao preprocessing comparison under one strong EDGRec mainline so the default preset can be chosen once and then left fixed.
KuaiRec_v2 preprocessing comparison across the sparse watch-ratio threshold views plus diagnostic raw and small-matrix full-observation views.
KuaiRand-1K preprocessing comparison between the causal-default and randomized-only evaluation views under the same EDGRec mainline.
AmazonBook follow-up: reuse the strong EDGRec learned-fusion setting and test a small popularity-weighted hard-negative fraction.
AmazonBook follow-up: same learned-fusion EDGRec setting with a stronger popularity-weighted hard-negative mix.
KuaiRand-1K diagnostic: switch to the randomized-exposure-only preset so Recall@K is measured on a much smaller, more interpretable future-positive set.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 256, cosine LR 0.01, shallow 1/1 branches, low fanout.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 512, cosine LR 0.01, shallow 1/1 branches, low fanout.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 1024, cosine LR 0.01, shallow 1/1 branches, low fanout.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 256, cosine LR 0.01, 1/2 branches, low and compact fanout.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 512, cosine LR 0.01, 1/2 branches, low and compact fanout.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 1024, cosine LR 0.01, 1/2 branches, low and compact fanout.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 256, constant LR 0.001, shallow 1/1 branches, low fanout.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 512, constant LR 0.001, shallow 1/1 branches, low fanout.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 1024, constant LR 0.001, shallow 1/1 branches, low fanout.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 256, constant LR 0.001, 1/2 branches, low and compact fanout.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 512, constant LR 0.001, 1/2 branches, low and compact fanout.
Fixed small-batch EDGRec probe: batch 1024, constant LR 0.001, 1/2 branches, low and compact fanout.

edgrec.description: EDGRec with mini-batch training on the default observed train-interaction graph.

lightgcn.description: Scalable sampled-neighborhood LightGCN approximation with mini-batch training on the default observed train-interaction graph.

lightgcn_paper.description: Paper-faithful LightGCN adapter with full-graph propagation, observed graph only, and no dropout.

dice_like.description: Legacy DICE-like ablation with mini-batch training; not the official DICE paper baseline.

dice_like_ablation.description: Explicit name for the legacy DICE-like ablation; use dice_paper for the paper baseline.

dice_paper.description: Paper-faithful GCN-DICE adapter with full-graph self-looped LightGCN propagation, DICE negative sampling, and DICE branch losses.
